title: Friday Sermon 21 July 1995
description:
In 'Therein you will have all that you will desire and therein you will have all that you will ask for -:

An entertainment from the Most Forgiving the Merciful.

('The Holy Quran'.  Ha Mim Sajdah: 32, 33).

Allah Almighty speaks of serving His guests and of the attribute of al-Ghufur [The Coverer] first of all.  Apparently this attribute does not seem to bear any relation to hospitality but wherever the subject of hospitality is mentioned in 'The Holy Quran' there absolution is also especially emphasised.  The mores Allah Almighty will adopt for the hospitality of His people are the most beautiful ways and we should adopt the same in the hospitality we offer and adopt the colours of Almighty God.

مَا تَشْتَهِي أَنفُسُكُمْ ['All that you will desire'] are the persistent natural habits which are a part of one's nature and it is the responsibility of the host to avail the guest of such things if he knows him.  The words مَا تَدَّعُونَ ['all that you will ask for'] speak of nearness and affection because only one to whom one can relate and with whom one is close and informal would ask for something and to create such an atmosphere demands high values.

In Arabic the expression Ikram e Zayf is used for hospitality which means that one treats one's guest in a way in which they feel honoured and one's hospitality is not dignified until one is respectful.  Allah Almighty mentions generosity of providence in relation to hospitality and states that 'I am bounteous and will provide what is bountiful' but with it He mentions forgiveness which means that one would reach only reach 'bountiful providence after passing through My forgiveness'.  One should also forgive the guests, in the things in which one has the power to forgive.  In hospitality many times such situations arise where one needs to forgive.  If an admonishment is to be made then do so with kindness.  In order to be hospitable one would have to be kind.  One would have to serve with courtesy.  A simple meagre service can also become generous if the host is courteous and offers it with courtesy and feeling and this has to do with the tendency of the host's heart.   If you are generous, if courteous values are imbued in your heart then whatever you will present before the guest would be considered bountiful.  

Serve the guests with the intent that their journey is for the sake of Allah and serve them with the hospitality with which Allah serves His guests and in this way you will also be included in those guests of Allah to whom He has promised forgiveness and a generous reward.  

To be honoured with God's hospitality it is important that you have those signs which God mentions about His guests.  According to many of the Quranic verses these are those whose hearts tremble when God is mentioned, in love of Allah and due to his majesty and power.  These are the ones who increase in faith when Allah's Signs are recited before them.  They trust in Allah, they establish prayer and spend out of what He has granted them.  Train yourselves, your children and your guests as well in this way.  Both the guests and the hosts are to establish prayer in any regard.  Try to keep the guests occupied in the Jalsa at most times.  Sit in during the speeches even if you cannot understand them at all because it is an audience for the sake of Allah.  I also urge the guests that the Holy Prophet ﷺ limited hospitality to 3 days and to charity thereafter.  So the guests should also remember this and avoid being a burden on their hosts.

I pray that Allah Almighty may enable all the hosts to do justice to serving the guests coming for the sake of Allah.  May Allah enable all those who are arriving to be true Muslim guests and enable those living here to prove true Muslim hosts.


The text https://www.alislam.org/urdu/pdf/Khutbat-e-Tahir-Vol-14-1995.pdf#page=525
